key_requirements: Proposed: civil remedy for non-consensual deepfakes; no federal criminal statute
notes: Multiple federal bills introduced including DEFIANCE Act and NO FAKES Act; none enacted as of early 2026. Enforcement falls to FTC for deceptive practices and state AGs

key_requirements: Prohibition on non-consensual deepfake intimate images; disclosure requirements for political deepfakes within 60 days of election
notes: California is the most active U.S. state regulator; AB 602 (2023) creates civil cause of action for non-consensual deepfakes; does not address fraud or authentication bypass

key_requirements: Prohibition on deepfakes depicting sexual conduct without consent; criminal penalties
notes: Criminal statute with penalties up to 2 years imprisonment; narrower scope than California; no civil right of action

key_requirements: High-risk AI transparency obligations; mandatory labeling of AI-generated content; deepfake watermarking; general-purpose AI systemic risk provisions
enforcement_body: EU AI Office / national competent authorities
notes: Most comprehensive binding framework; risk-tiered approach; enforcement by EU AI Office with national implementation; significant extraterritorial reach for providers serving EU market. Full enforcement phased through 2027

key_requirements: Mandatory real-name registration for deepfake creation; content labeling required; platform liability for user-generated synthetic media; prohibited uses include political manipulation and fraud
notes: Among most assertive national frameworks; comprehensive labeling requirements; but enforcement applied inconsistently and primarily in politically sensitive contexts per international observers. Limited transparency on enforcement statistics

key_requirements: Illegal content (CSAM deepfakes criminalized); harmful content duties for platforms; deepfake intimate imagery sharing made criminal offense under Criminal Justice Bill 2024
notes: Online Safety Act creates positive platform duties; Criminal Justice Bill (2024) adds specific deepfake intimate imagery offense; no general AI content transparency requirement yet

key_requirements: Existing criminal law applied to some deepfake misuse; Online Safety Act covers some harmful content; no deepfake-specific transparency or watermarking requirements
notes: Regulatory review underway as of 2026; Australian Law Reform Commission examining deepfake harms; no comprehensive framework enacted
